It's been a delight to provide small amounts of advice and suggestions to people working on the Decoupled DiLoCo training system.  This approach enables graceful handling of failures in large scale training jobs, by allowing (N-1) / N units to proceed when one fails.

If you can’t stop small teams from using your API for distillation, then you’re definitely not stopping criminals, biohackers, or adversarial states from using AI through it. Those actors are much more sophisticated. The whole "APIs are safer" is a hoax!

Among all the model release furor, important to note that people don’t need to switch providers or declare a winner every time a new model is released.

Especially as Opus 4.7 is a good model too! (Especially since adaptive thinking got better)
Crazy to me that GPT-5.5 is 2-12x cheaper than the original GPT-3 API pricing. What a wonder of science!

Congratulations to @countzerozzz (my manager in Gemini and long-term mentor) for getting the well deserved Test of Time award! DDPG inspired my whole PhD work. And ofc to great friends and mentors @tom_erez @yuvaltassa, and David Silver (whom I defended my thesis against).

A hill that I will die on: with today's AI models, intelligence is a function of inference compute. Comparing models by a single number hasn't made sense since 2024. What matters is intelligence per token or per $.

This is especially true when using it in a product like Codex.

Codex app is getting super powerful for coding, and any work on your computer.

- In app browser & browser use—great for testing changes
- Great Excel/Sheets/Powerpoint/Slides generation
- Imagegen2 insane in the mix
- Auto-review for long tasks & automations

GPT-5.5 is a new class of intelligence.

This intelligence makes it intuitive to use; it completes challenging tasks with little micromanagement. Also very token efficient, and runs with low latency and at scale.

A real step toward a new way of getting computer work done.
GPT-5.5 is now available on ChatGPT and Codex.
